competitors.name: KnowBe4
competitors.entry_point: KSAT Admin Console > Phishing > Vishing Security Tests (Gold tier and above)
competitors.setup_friction: CSV upload of phone numbers required — not sourced from directory or group system. Separate from email phishing targeting.
core_flow: Upload CSV with employee phone numbers
Select VST template (5 Kevin Mitnick scenarios or custom TTS)
competitors.error_prevention: None described. No compliance pre-flight, no consent check, no preview before launch.
competitors.trust_model: Minimal. No pre-flight review step, no acknowledgment, immediate launch after setup.
competitors.rbac_notes: Gold tier required. No specific sub-admin role differentiation for vishing described.
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Set-and-forget recurring frequency. Templates reusable. CSV must be re-uploaded to update targeting.
competitors.ai_personalization: None. Pre-recorded or TTS messages only. No adaptive conversation.
competitors.remediation_path: Fail triggers short remedial training module assignment.
competitors.source_notes: Support documentation and press release directly observed. IVR execution and binary outcome confirmed.

competitors.name: Adaptive Security
competitors.entry_point: Platform > Phishing Simulations > Voice (within automated campaign program)
competitors.setup_friction: Executive voice persona requires audio recording upload. Multi-channel scenario setup spans email + voice + SMS configuration.
core_flow: Define attack goal and target role
Select or create voice persona (exec impersonation or generic)
Configure scenario context and social engineering tactics
Set role-based targeting and adaptive difficulty
feedback_states: Behavioral outcome metrics (engagement patterns, information disclosure)
competitors.trust_model: Medium. Always-on continuous execution — admins configure once, campaigns run indefinitely. Limited described pre-launch review.
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: High — always-on model. No per-campaign relaunch needed. Role-based targeting updates dynamically.
competitors.ai_personalization: Strong. Executive voice cloning via audio upload, OSINT-driven scenario personalization, adaptive difficulty per user.
competitors.risk_score_surface: Strong. Simulation outcomes feed directly into human risk scores.
competitors.remediation_path: Automated remediation training triggers on failure.
competitors.source_notes: Product page and blog observed. Campaign configuration workflow inferred — not directly observed in admin console.

competitors.name: Hoxhunt
competitors.entry_point: Not a self-serve feature. Accessed via engagement with Hoxhunt team as a custom managed service.
competitors.setup_friction: Very high. Admin cannot configure or launch independently. Hoxhunt team builds scenario using executive voice/likeness.
core_flow: Executive voice and video sample captured
Hoxhunt delivers campaign on agreed timeline
Results returned to admin in platform
competitors.error_prevention: Managed by vendor team — admin has no pre-flight visibility into scenario before delivery.
competitors.trust_model: Low admin control. Admins have no direct visibility into what scenario will be used or when until post-delivery.
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Low. Each engagement is custom and requires vendor involvement.
competitors.ai_personalization: High capability — deepfake voice and video. But admin has no control over persona configuration.
competitors.source_notes: Managed service nature confirmed across multiple sources (blog, buyer's guide). Self-serve vishing absence confirmed.

competitors.name: SoSafe
competitors.entry_point: Not a self-serve recurring campaign. One-off demo experience for executive buy-in.
core_flow: SoSafe team executes one-off personalized vishing call
competitors.ai_personalization: Personalized scenario using public data, but admin has no config control.
competitors.source_notes: Early access status and demo-only nature confirmed from product news. Limited workflow detail.

competitors.name: Proofpoint
competitors.setup_friction: N/A — vishing not available as self-serve feature.
competitors.source_notes: Absence confirmed from product documentation review. Voice vishing not a documented core feature of Proofpoint SAT (ZenGuide).

observed_patterns: Automated VOIP/AI execution is the market norm — all self-serve platforms use automation, not human operators
Time window configuration (business hours default with admin override) is a recognized, expected concept
Role-based or group-based targeting is expected — CSV upload is a below-market anti-pattern
Immediate remediation coupling on failure is the default in awareness training; red team suppression is an intentional departure
Binary pass/fail outcomes are the current market floor for vishing — no competitor has multi-state behavioral classification
Compliance gating is absent from all competitor products
anti_patterns: CSV upload for phone number targeting — forces duplicate data management separate from user directory
Binary pass/fail outcomes for red team context — inadequate for measuring adversarial engagement depth
Siloing vishing as a separate product section or add-on from other red team channels
Always-on execution without explicit admin control points — removes visibility into when campaigns fire, problematic for debrief scheduling
No compliance surface at all — creates legal risk for enterprise and EMEA customers
differentiation_opportunities: Richest outcome taxonomy in market: 5-state model (Not Yet Called, No Answer, Engaged, Compromised, Declined) vs. binary pass/fail across all competitors

Voice AEP as structured Persona + Scenario builder — Persona step (voice, greeting, banned phrases, tone) + Scenario step (phases, collectibles, tactics, goals) with live call flow visualization is architecturally unlike anything in the market

OSINT-grounded scenario personalization — Facts/OSINT system injects company-specific intelligence (CISO name, EDR product, wire policy, funding news) into the AI at runtime; no competitor has this; organizational intelligence deepens over time making calls increasingly convincing

Admin OSINT review step (Step 3.5) — makes AI realism transparent and admin-accountable; unique concept that no competitor has; frames as 'here's what the AI knows about your company' rather than a black box
Compliance-aware pre-flight wizard with consent, jurisdiction, and works council gating — no competitor has this
Unified red team campaign launcher (vishing + SMS + WhatsApp in one wizard + one reporting surface) — competitors silo vishing
Live call monitoring during campaign with per-call state tracking — no competitor has described equivalent in-progress view for vishing
Request model with explicit debrief responsibility — admin stays informed and in control vs. opaque managed service
design_implications: Set execution model expectations at Step 1 channel card — automated AI execution is the market default and admins will assume it correctly for Dune
Compliance pre-flight UI must visually distinguish platform-verified states from self-certified acknowledgments — novel concept with no market precedent
Outcome states need first-use education — red team buyers may be conditioned to binary pass/fail from prior tools
Voice AEP Channel Type selector must preview the 3-step structure (Persona → Scenario → Test & Refine) and hint at the scenario visualization before admin commits
OSINT review step must feel like an intelligence briefing ('here's what the AI knows about your company'), not a compliance gate — this is where Dune's differentiation is most tangible to the admin

Scenario visualization is the most important comprehension tool — admins reviewing a library AEP for the first time must be able to understand the call's entire playbook from the diagram without reading YAML
Reporting must use vishing-specific charts that signal purpose-built surface, not repurposed phishing dashboard
Recommend adding Brightside AI to competitor-list.md — most sophisticated vishing-specific platform observed, not currently in registry
confidence_notes: KnowBe4: High — support docs and press release directly observed
Adaptive Security: Medium — product page and blog observed; admin console workflow inferred
Hoxhunt: High — managed service nature confirmed across multiple independent sources
SoSafe: Medium — demo-only nature confirmed; limited workflow detail
Proofpoint: High — absence of vishing as documented feature confirmed
Brightside AI (non-registry): Observed for market context only — most complete vishing buyer's guide patterns came from their content; recommend adding to competitor list
